steps.title: Read the context
steps.content: Antonia starts with $130$ and earns $2.5\%$ more each year. One year later the amount is $130(1.025)=133.25$. Enter $x_1=[0,1]$, $y_1=[130,133.25]$, and $y_1\sim a b^{x_1}$ in Desmos.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Decreasing exponential: The amount grows each year (2.5% more), so it is not decreasing. ✗
  • Choice B — Decreasing linear: Again, the amount is increasing, not decreasing. ✗
  • Choice C — Increasing exponential: Each year the increase is 2.5% of the current amount — a constant multiplier of $1.025$. Since the amount being multiplied grows year over year, each year's increase is larger than the last. This is exponential growth, and the amount increases. ✓
  • Choice D — Increasing linear: A linear model would add the same fixed dollar amount each year. But 2.5% of a growing balance adds more each year, so the growth is not constant. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
